feature: Full project architecture analysis and technical debt audit
workflow_rationale: This is a read-only codebase audit producing analysis reports. No functional code changes. The investigation workflow fits because we are: (1) discovering architecture, (2) analyzing SOLID/OOP violations, (3) producing a technical debt report, and (4) hardening with a refactoring roadmap. Output is structured knowledge, not code.

phases.name: Architecture Discovery & Mapping
phases.description: Map the complete project architecture: layers, modules, dependencies, data flow, and extension points. Builds on existing .planning/codebase/ docs but goes deeper with a dependency graph and data flow analysis.

subtasks.description: Create comprehensive architecture map: document all layers (Claude Code hooks -> Bash wrappers -> Python lib -> WorldGraph), map module-to-module dependencies, trace data flow from user action through tool -> lib -> world.json, and document the middleware dispatch system. Output: ARCHITECTURE-MAP.md in .planning/codebase/

output_sections: Module dependency graph (all 19 core + 6 RAG modules)
Data flow traces for 3 representative operations
Entry point map (all 23 bash tools -> lib modules)

verification.instructions: Verify ARCHITECTURE-MAP.md exists in .planning/codebase/, covers all 19 core modules in dependency graph, documents the 4 architectural layers, includes at least 3 data flow traces, and maps all 23 tool -> lib relationships

subtasks.notes: Created ARCHITECTURE-MAP.md covering all 4 layers, 23 tool-to-lib mappings, 19 core modules dependency graph, 5 data flow traces, middleware dispatch system, and optional modules documentation.

subtasks.description: Map all module interactions and middleware dispatch chains: document which tools use dispatch_middleware (8 tools), which modules provide middleware hooks, how pre/post hooks interact, and document the module enable/disable lifecycle. Include the 3 optional modules (world-travel, mass-combat, firearms-combat) and their middleware.

analysis_scope.note: Output is included in ARCHITECTURE-MAP.md as a dedicated section

verification.instructions: Verify the module interaction section exists in ARCHITECTURE-MAP.md, lists all 8 middleware-enabled tools, documents all 3 modules' middleware hooks, and identifies any conflict risks

subtasks.notes: Documented all 10 middleware-enabled CORE tools, all 4 modules' middleware hooks (custom-stats: 9 files, world-travel: 2, mass-combat/firearms-combat: 0), dispatch chain detail, enable/disable lifecycle, conflict risks, and coverage matrix in ARCHITECTURE-MAP.md.

phases.name: Critical God Class Deep Analysis
phases.description: Deep-dive into the 3 most critical files: WorldGraph (2,481 LOC, 87 methods), InventoryManager (1,559 LOC, 38 methods), and the EntityManager base class (172 LOC). Produce detailed method catalogs and concrete decomposition proposals.

subtasks.description: Deep analysis of WorldGraph god class (lib/world_graph.py, 2,481 LOC, 87 methods). Catalog every method by responsibility group (node CRUD, edge CRUD, graph queries, inventory ops, domain-specific ops, tick engine, formatting, CLI). For each group: count methods, total LOC, coupling to other groups. Propose concrete decomposition into 4-6 focused classes with clear boundaries, migration path, and backward compatibility strategy. Output: GOD-CLASS-DECOMPOSITION.md section 1.

output_sections: Method catalog grouped by responsibility (with line ranges)
Responsibility group metrics (method count, LOC per group)
Proposed decomposition: NodeManager, EdgeManager, GraphQuery, TickEngine, WorldGraphCLI
Impact on existing tests and tools

verification.instructions: Verify GOD-CLASS-DECOMPOSITION.md has WorldGraph section with: (1) complete method catalog covering all 87 methods, (2) responsibility groups with LOC counts, (3) specific decomposition proposal with class names and method assignments, (4) migration strategy

subtasks.notes: Created GOD-CLASS-DECOMPOSITION.md Section 1: WorldGraph analysis with complete 87-method catalog across 16 responsibility groups, inter-group coupling matrix, 5-class decomposition proposal (GraphStore, WorldDomain, InventoryEngine, TickEngine, WorldGraph facade), incremental migration strategy, and backward compatibility plan.

subtasks.description: Deep analysis of InventoryManager god class (lib/inventory_manager.py, 1,559 LOC, 38 methods). Catalog all methods by responsibility group (load/save, item ops, transactions, weight/encumbrance, display, crafting/use, wiki integration). Propose decomposition into focused classes. Also analyze the dual player/NPC mode and its implications. Output: GOD-CLASS-DECOMPOSITION.md section 2.

output_sections: Method catalog grouped by responsibility (with line ranges)
Proposed decomposition: ItemManager, TransactionManager, EncumbranceCalculator, InventoryDisplay, CraftingEngine

verification.instructions: Verify GOD-CLASS-DECOMPOSITION.md has InventoryManager section with: (1) complete method catalog for all 38 methods, (2) decomposition proposal with clear class boundaries, (3) player/NPC mode analysis, (4) migration strategy

subtasks.notes: Completed InventoryManager analysis: 38 methods cataloged across 8 responsibility groups, dual player/NPC mode analysis, 7 code smells identified, 5-class decomposition proposed (CharacterStore, WeightCalculator, InventoryTransaction, InventoryDisplay, InventoryManager facade), phased migration strategy.

subtasks.description: Deep analysis of secondary problem classes: DiceRoller (lib/dice.py, 777 LOC, 300+ line main()), EntityEnhancer (lib/entity_enhancer.py, 877 LOC, dual responsibility), AgentExtractor (lib/agent_extractor.py, 760 LOC, sys.exit in lib). For each: document the specific violations, propose extraction/decomposition, and assess the sys.exit() removal in agent_extractor. Output: GOD-CLASS-DECOMPOSITION.md section 3.

output_sections: DiceRoller: main() decomposition into DiceCLI, CharacterLoader, CombatResolver
EntityEnhancer: split RAG search from graph search into two services
AgentExtractor: sys.exit() removal plan, error propagation strategy

verification.instructions: Verify GOD-CLASS-DECOMPOSITION.md section 3 covers all 3 files with specific violation line ranges, decomposition proposals, and effort estimates

phases.name: Systematic SOLID & Quality Audit
phases.description: Systematically audit all remaining modules (medium/low priority) for SOLID violations, code smells, and quality issues. Catalog every violation with file, line range, principle violated, severity, and recommended fix.

subtasks.description: Audit core manager modules for SOLID violations: EntityManager (172 LOC), PlayerManager (611 LOC), SessionManager (541 LOC), CampaignManager (445 LOC), TimeManager (258 LOC). For each: identify SRP violations, DIP violations (hard dependency creation), OCP violations (non-extensible designs), and catalog magic numbers/hardcoded values. Output: SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md section 1 (Manager Modules).

analysis_scope.output_format: Table per file: | Line Range | Principle | Severity | Violation Description | Recommended Fix |

verification.instructions: Verify SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md section 1 has analysis tables for all 5 manager modules, each with specific line ranges and severities

subtasks.notes: Created SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md Section 1 with analysis tables for all 5 manager modules. Found 38 violations (10 high, 22 medium, 6 low). Key issues: PlayerManager SRP violations (611 LOC god-class), SessionManager 4-responsibility god-class, CLI mixed with domain logic in all managers, hardcoded D&D 5e rules.

subtasks.description: Audit utility modules for SOLID violations and code smells: json_ops (284 LOC), validators (303 LOC), currency (234 LOC), calendar (258 LOC), colors (314 LOC), module_data (80 LOC), encounter_engine (236 LOC), extraction_schemas (179 LOC), content_extractor (294 LOC). Focus on: duplicated logic, magic numbers, error handling anti-patterns, and OCP violations. Output: SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md section 2 (Utility Modules).

analysis_scope.output_format: Table per file: | Line Range | Principle | Severity | Violation Description | Recommended Fix |

verification.instructions: Verify SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md section 2 has analysis tables for all 9 utility modules with specific line ranges

subtasks.notes: Audited 9 utility modules (2,093 LOC). Found 42 SOLID violations (7 High, 27 Medium, 8 Low). Key findings: validators.py 9× copy-pasted methods, json_ops.py 5× duplicated path nav, encounter_engine.py 73-line god function. Section 2 added to SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md.

subtasks.description: Audit RAG subsystem (6 modules, ~1,357 LOC): vector_store (307 LOC), rag_extractor (275 LOC), semantic_chunker (262 LOC), quote_extractor (226 LOC), extraction_queries (190 LOC), embedder (176 LOC). Focus on: error handling (stderr suppression in embedder), hardcoded thresholds (semantic_chunker similarity), coupling between RAG components, and optional dependency handling (chromadb/sentence-transformers may not be installed). Output: SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md section 3 (RAG Subsystem).

analysis_scope.output_format: Table per file: | Line Range | Principle | Severity | Violation Description | Recommended Fix |

verification.instructions: Verify SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md section 3 covers all 6 RAG modules with specific line ranges and includes analysis of optional dependency handling

subtasks.notes: Audited all 6 RAG modules (1,423 LOC). Found 32 violations: 2 High (stderr suppression in embedder, god-method in quote_extractor), 20 Medium, 10 Low. Key issues: sys.stderr replacement hiding errors, hardcoded similarity thresholds (0.35, 1.3), incomplete optional dependency guards, print-based progress in all modules, D&D-specific query templates.

subtasks.description: Audit the 3 optional modules: world-travel (~6,942 LOC, 13 lib files), mass-combat (~1,225 LOC), firearms-combat (~699 LOC). Focus on: module isolation (do they leak campaign-specific content?), middleware correctness (pre/post hook semantics), inter-module dependency risks, and adherence to the module development pattern. Output: SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md section 4 (Optional Modules).

analysis_scope.note: Read module.json + main lib file for each module; skim middleware files
analysis_scope.output_format: Table per module: | Component | Severity | Issue | Recommended Fix |

verification.instructions: Verify SOLID-VIOLATIONS.md section 4 covers all 3 optional modules, checks campaign-agnosticism, and documents middleware correctness

subtasks.notes: Audited all 3 optional modules. Found 23 SOLID violations (5 High, 12 Medium, 6 Low). Key findings: 8 campaign-agnosticism violations including hardcoded Russian "Стрелок" in firearms-combat production code, fragile regex stdout parsing in world-travel middleware, no cross-module dependencies.

phases.name: Cross-Cutting Concerns Analysis
phases.description: Analyze cross-cutting concerns that span all modules: error handling patterns, dependency injection assessment, configuration debt (magic numbers), and test coverage gaps. Each produces a dedicated analysis document.

subtasks.description: Error handling audit across ALL modules. For each file in lib/ and lib/rag/: catalog every print-based error, every silent failure (bare except, return default), every broad exception catch, every sys.exit() call. Produce a file-by-file table. Propose an exception hierarchy (DMError -> EntityNotFoundError, ValidationError, ConfigError, FileError, RAGError). Document which modules need the most work. Output: ERROR-HANDLING-AUDIT.md

output_sections: Silent failure catalog (return default on error)
Proposed exception hierarchy with class diagram
Migration priority (which files to fix first)

subtasks.description: Dependency injection assessment. For each class in lib/: document how it creates its dependencies (direct construction vs parameter injection). Map the full dependency graph showing which classes create which others. Propose a DI strategy: (1) Protocol/ABC interfaces for key services, (2) Constructor injection pattern, (3) Factory functions for default wiring. Include concrete before/after code examples. Output: DI-ASSESSMENT.md

output_sections: Current dependency creation table (class -> creates -> dependencies)
Before/after code examples for EntityManager DI refactoring

subtasks.description: Configuration debt catalog. Search all lib/ files for: magic numbers (hardcoded ints/floats used as thresholds), magic strings (hardcoded directory names, file names, format strings), and values that should be configurable per-campaign. Group by category: XP thresholds, encounter rates, file paths, validation limits, color thresholds, similarity scores, format strings. Propose centralized config strategy. Output: CONFIG-DEBT.md

search_patterns: Hardcoded strings (file names, directory names, format patterns)
output_sections: Magic numbers catalog: | File | Line | Value | Purpose | Recommended Externalization |
Magic strings catalog: | File | Line | String | Purpose |

subtasks.description: Test coverage gap analysis. For each untested module (13/19 core, 6/6 RAG): assess testability (how hard to test given current coupling), propose specific test cases (method-level), estimate LOC for test file, and prioritize by risk. Also analyze existing test quality: do tests cover edge cases? Are fixtures adequate? Is mocking appropriate? Output: TEST-COVERAGE-GAPS.md

output_sections: Current coverage matrix: | Module | Tested? | Test File | Test Methods | Edge Cases? |
Untested module risk assessment: | Module | LOC | Risk | Testability | Blocking Issue |
Proposed test cases per untested module (method-level)
Estimated effort: | Module | Est. Test LOC | Effort | Priority |
Existing test quality review (fixtures, mocking, edge cases)

phases.name: Report Consolidation & Refactoring Roadmap
phases.description: Consolidate all findings from phases 2-4 into a master technical debt report and a phased refactoring roadmap. Verify all findings against actual code. Run existing tests to confirm no regressions.

subtasks.description: Create the master TECHNICAL-DEBT-REPORT.md consolidating all findings from phases 2-4. Structure: Executive Summary (top 10 findings), Quality Scorecard (current vs target), SOLID Violations Summary (by severity), God Class Status, Error Handling Score, DI Assessment, Config Debt Score, Test Coverage Score. Include counts and metrics. Reference detailed findings in other documents. Output: TECHNICAL-DEBT-REPORT.md

output_sections: Quality Scorecard table (8 categories, current vs target)
SOLID violations count by principle and severity
God class metrics (method count, LOC, coupling score)
Error handling score (% print-based vs exception-based)
Config debt count (magic numbers, strings, lists)
Test coverage score (% modules tested, % methods tested)

verification.instructions: Verify TECHNICAL-DEBT-REPORT.md has: (1) Executive summary with top 10 findings, (2) Quality scorecard, (3) Metrics for all 8 analysis categories, (4) Cross-references to all other analysis documents

subtasks.description: Create the REFACTORING-ROADMAP.md: a phased, prioritized plan for addressing all identified technical debt. Phases: (1) Quick Wins - 1-2 day effort, low risk (centralize config, add logging, fix sys.exit), (2) DI & Testability - 3-5 day effort, medium risk (Protocol interfaces, constructor injection, factory functions), (3) God Class Decomposition - 5-10 day effort, high risk (WorldGraph split, InventoryManager split), (4) Test Coverage - ongoing, low risk (priority-ordered test creation), (5) Error Handling Overhaul - 3-5 day effort, medium risk (exception hierarchy, replace prints). Each phase has: effort estimate, risk assessment, dependencies, success criteria, and rollback strategy. Output: REFACTORING-ROADMAP.md

output_sections: Phase 3: God Class Decomposition (specific tasks, 5-10 days)
Phase 4: Test Coverage Expansion (priority order, ongoing)
Phase 5: Error Handling Overhaul (specific tasks, 3-5 days)

verification.instructions: Verify REFACTORING-ROADMAP.md has: (1) 5 phased sections with effort estimates, (2) dependency graph, (3) risk assessments, (4) success criteria for each phase, (5) total timeline estimate

subtasks.description: Final verification: (1) Run existing test suite to confirm no regressions (no code was modified). (2) Cross-check all LOC counts, method counts, and file references in all analysis documents against actual source files. (3) Verify all analysis files are English-only (language policy). (4) Ensure no source code was modified (git status clean). (5) Run ruff check to document baseline lint status.

verification.command: cd /mnt/data/Projects/Python/Claude-Code-Game-Master && uv run pytest && echo '---TESTS PASSED---' && git status --short && echo '---GIT STATUS---'
verification.expected: All tests pass, no source files modified (only .planning/ files may appear as new)

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on phase-1, analyze different file sets (critical god classes vs remaining modules), no file overlap in outputs (different sections of different documents)

parallelism.speedup_estimate: ~1.4x faster than sequential (phases 2 and 3 overlap, saving ~2-3 subtasks worth of time)

acceptance_criteria: All 19 core lib modules appear in the analysis
All 6 RAG modules appear in the analysis
All 3 optional modules appear in the analysis
Every SOLID violation has file, line range, severity, and recommended fix
God class decomposition proposals are concrete (named classes, assigned methods)
Test coverage gap report covers all 13 untested core modules
Refactoring roadmap has phased plan with effort estimates
No source code modified (git status clean for lib/, tools/)
Existing tests still pass (uv run pytest)

verification_steps.name: Existing Tests Pass
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All tests pass - no regressions from analysis

verification_steps.name: Baseline Lint Check
verification_steps.command: uv run ruff check lib/ 2>&1 | tail -5

verification_strategy.reasoning: Low risk investigation task producing analysis artifacts only. No functional code changes. Minimal validation to confirm existing tests pass and no accidental source modifications.

unit_tests.note: Only verifying existing tests still pass - no new tests created in this task

checks: LOC counts in reports match actual file sizes (within 5%)
All SOLID violations reference real code patterns with line numbers
Decomposition proposals name specific classes with method assignments
Refactoring roadmap has 5 phases with effort estimates
No source code files were modified
